bullets.text: Number 10 has stated that the Falklands' sovereignty remains with the UK, following reports of a US 'review' which suggested a potential shift in position as retaliation for the UK not joining the Iran war.
A key suspect in the notorious Tadamon massacre during the Syria civil war, Amjad Youssef, has been arrested. He was wanted over the mass killing of blindfolded civilians in April 2013 in Damascus.
Democracy is increasingly threatened by the climate crisis, with new analysis documenting that natural hazards linked to climate change disrupted 23 elections in 18 countries in 2024.

Anzac Day services were held across Australia, with thousands gathering in Canberra for the 111th anniversary of the Gallipoli landings, and Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and New Zealand High Commissioner Andrew Needs among those present.
A Queensland Ombudsman report has revealed instances where prison inmates accused of sexually assaulting cellmates continued to share cells with other prisoners.
The Victorian government has announced an initial $860 million investment to build 7,000 additional social housing properties over the next decade.
US stocks, particularly in the tech sector, have reached all-time highs, fueled by a strong sales forecast from Intel Corp. and optimism surrounding potential US-Iran talks.
Google is set to invest up to $40 billion in Anthropic, an AI lab, to help it expand its computing power for running AI models.
The Brazilian government has blocked access to Polymarket and Kalshi, two prediction market platforms, citing non-compliance with federal gambling laws.
